note: The module adds a role-visibility setting to menu_link_content links and enforces it by overriding the core menu tree manipulator. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with Menu Item Role Access enabled, where do I choose which roles can see a particular menu link?
evals.expected_output: On the menu link add/edit form (menu_link_content), a 'Menu Item Roles' checkboxes field lets you pick the roles allowed to see that link.

evals.prompt: If I select the 'editor' role on a menu link with Menu Item Role Access, what happens for a user who is not an editor?
evals.expected_output: Users who do not hold at least one selected role do not see the link; the tree manipulator returns AccessResult::forbidden() for them.

evals.prompt: With Menu Item Role Access, what happens to a menu link if I leave its roles field empty?
evals.expected_output: An empty menu_item_roles field adds no restriction, so the link is visible to everyone as normal.

evals.prompt: In Menu Item Role Access, on which entity and field are the allowed roles for a menu link stored?
evals.expected_output: In the menu_item_roles base field (entity_reference to user_role) added to the menu_link_content entity.

evals.prompt: How does Menu Item Role Access actually enforce which roles see a menu link on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: It overrides the core menu.default_tree_manipulators service with MenuItemRoleAccessLinkTreeManipulator (extends DefaultMenuLinkTreeManipulators) and adds the role check in menuLinkCheckAccess().

evals.prompt: Does Menu Item Role Access secure the destination page of a restricted menu link on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: No. It only controls whether the link is displayed (visibility), not page access; the target route's own permissions still apply.

evals.prompt: In Menu Item Role Access, which setting makes the role selection alone decide link visibility, ignoring the target route's own access check? Give the config key.
evals.expected_output: The overwrite_internal_link_target_access key (default false) on menu_item_role_access.config.

evals.prompt: Which permission lets a user edit the role field on menu links in Menu Item Role Access, versus administer the module's settings?
evals.expected_output: 'edit menu_item_role_access' gates editing the menu_item_roles field; 'administer menu_item_role_access' gates the settings/config page.
